argument: First, verify 83(b) status immediately — Stripe Atlas auto-files, so manual submission risked duplication (Class 19). This EIN confirms entity existence; do not act until confirming filing ownership. Second, trigger Mercury onboarding: EIN unlocks business account, but only after ledger check — Class 21 demands we track financial obligations explicitly. Third, assess franchise tax deadline: DE corp, incorporation April 14, due by March 1 annually — next due March 1, 2027. But no action today. Priority is safeguarding against recurrence of $150 loss. Do not assume; verify. The 83(b) must be checked against Stripe Atlas status *before* any filing is considered. If we skip this, we repeat Class 19. If we miss franchise tax, penalty accrues — but not yet. If we don’t log this in `atlas-debts-to-ceo.md`, we repeat Class 21.
strongest_counter: Others may argue to file franchise tax now or open Mercury immediately — but verification has higher leverage. Acting without truth is regression.
evidence_cited: Class 21 — recurring financial loss without ledger
ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION.md Law 1: NEVER RED (implies error prevention over speed)
